name: webpilot-server-for-chrome-extension
description: MCP server for browser control via Chrome extension

scripts.test: node test/formatter-diagnostics.test.js && node test/platform-guide-gate.test.js && node test/formatter-updater-ref.test.js
scripts.build: echo Use build:win, build:mac, or build:linux && exit 1
scripts.build:win: npm run build:web-ui && pkg . --target node22-win-x64 --out-path dist && node scripts/copy-native-deps.js
scripts.build:mac: npm run build:web-ui && pkg . --target node22-macos-x64 --out-path dist && node scripts/copy-native-deps.js
scripts.build:linux: npm run build:web-ui && pkg . --target node22-linux-x64 --out-path dist && node scripts/copy-native-deps.js

pkg._comment_native_modules: better-sqlite3 has a native binding (build/Release/better_sqlite3.node). @yao-pkg/pkg cannot bundle native .node files into the snapshot — they must be shipped as loose files next to the pkg-built .exe. Hoisted under monorepo node_modules at: <repo>/node_modules/better-sqlite3/build/Release/better_sqlite3.node. The Electron deploy step (packages/electron/) already copies the server dist + sidecar files into %LOCALAPPDATA%\WebPilot; extend that step to also copy the .node binary next to the .exe. better-sqlite3's loader (node_modules/better-sqlite3/lib/database.js) auto-discovers the .node binary alongside the running executable.
